name: open-scholar-skill
description: AI-assisted academic paper writing for social sciences. 23 skills + 1 utility + 9 peer-reviewer agents + 4 verification agents covering the modular research pipeline: idea formalization (5-agent evaluation panel), research question generation from codebooks/datasets, systematic literature review (PRISMA 2020), theory and hypothesis development (25+ frameworks), research design with power analysis (multilevel/DiD/RD/mediation/SEM) and computational study design, exploratory data analysis with missing-data diagnostics (condition number, DFBETAS, panel diagnostics), causal inference toolkit (DAGs + 13 identification strategies: OLS/DiD/staggered DiD/RD/IV/FE/matching/synthetic control/mediation/DML/causal forests/bunching/Bartik IV + distributional methods), data analytics with publication-quality tables and figures (11 outcome types, MI/Rubin's rules, GMM, Bayesian brms, LCA, SEM), computational social science (NLP/text-as-data with STM/BERTopic/conText/LLM annotation/DSL/NER/coreference, ML/DML/causal forests/conformal prediction, GNN/node embedding, network analysis with ERGM/temporal ERGM/SAOMs/stochastic block models/ego-network, ABM with Mesa/NetLogo/LLM agents, computer vision, geospatial, audio-as-data), sociolinguistics and discourse analysis (variationist/CA/CDA/computational/experimental/Biber MDA/voice quality), qualitative methods (grounded theory/thematic analysis/content analysis/LLM-assisted coding with human validation/mixed-methods integration/inter-rater reliability), section-by-section paper drafting with knowledge base and snippet library (appendix/SI structure, word budgets, CRediT template), citation management with 5-tier verification (Zotero/CrossRef/Semantic Scholar/OpenAlex/WebSearch) and semantic duplicate detection, journal formatting for 22 journals, open science (preregistration/data sharing/Registered Reports/FAIR/restricted data templates), replication package construction (6 modes with AEA README template), simulated peer review with 3-7 journal-calibrated reviewer agents (9 agent types: quant/theory/senior/computational/qual/ling/demographics/mixed-methods/ethics) and desk-reject risk assessment, multi-author collaboration (CRediT/tasks/mentoring), data safety with international restricted data markers and GDPR, research ethics (AI disclosure/plagiarism/integrity), two-stage analysis-to-manuscript verification (4-agent panel), continuous quality engine (4 modes with diagnostic-to-action mapping). Targets ASR, AJS, Demography, Science Advances, Nature Human Behaviour, Nature Computational Science.
